vendor: TurboPack, SynEdit, HtmlViewer, Delphi HTML Components
description: Conservative detection/review pack for widely used legacy and open-source Delphi component libraries. This pack helps identify SynEdit, HtmlViewer, TurboPower Orpheus, Abbrevia, AsyncPro, OnGuard, and related components; it avoids unsafe automatic conversion of complex VCL controls.

rules.notes: Plain text can be represented by TMemo, but syntax highlighting and editor behavior do not map safely.
rules.manual_review_reason: Review highlighters, gutter, bookmarks, completion, search, keyboard handling, and possible FMX editor replacement.
SynEdit memo behavior requires syntax/editor feature review.
Code completion popup/editor integration requires manual migration.
Exporter components may be preservable as nonvisual code but require package and output-path review.
HtmlViewer is VCL/Lazarus-oriented in common use; review whether to replace with FMX TWebBrowser, Delphi HTML Components, or another FMX HTML renderer.
Frame-based HTML viewing requires manual FMX replacement review.
HTML text rendering cannot be safely flattened to a plain FMX label without visual review.
HTML-enabled panel behavior requires an FMX-capable HTML renderer or manual redesign.
HTML-enabled item templates require manual FMX list/template migration.
Orpheus controls are legacy VCL controls; simple button behavior may map, but visual/style behavior needs review.
Orpheus edit masks, validation, and formatting require manual review.
Orpheus table/grid behavior requires manual migration.
Notebook/page behavior may map structurally, but legacy control behavior requires review.
Calendar selection and formatting behavior requires review.
Abbrevia compression component may be preservable as nonvisual code, but package/platform support and file paths must be reviewed.
Abbrevia visual archive browser requires manual FMX UI replacement.
Abbrevia visual archive view requires manual FMX UI replacement.
AsyncPro serial/TAPI behavior is platform-specific and requires manual migration.
AsyncPro protocol/data packet behavior requires package and threading review.
Terminal emulation UI requires manual FMX replacement.
OnGuard licensing/key components may be preservable but require security and package review.
OnGuard licensing behavior should be reviewed carefully during platform migration.
